# AI Provenance Protocol (APP) — Complete Reference

> An open standard for recording, embedding, and verifying the provenance of AI-generated content.
> Version: 1.0.0-draft | License: CC BY 4.0 (spec), Apache 2.0 (code)
> Website: https://aiprovenanceprotocol.io
> GitHub: https://github.com/AI-Provenance-Protocol/ai-provenance-protocol

## What is APP?

APP defines a vendor-neutral, machine-readable format for AI content provenance. It provides:
1. A JSON metadata schema (`_ai_provenance` key) with 5 required fields
2. Three embedding modes: inline JSON, HTTP headers, linked metadata
3. A verification protocol for third-party provenance confirmation

APP is designed to comply with the EU AI Act Article 50 (enforceable 2 August 2026).

## Core Schema — Required Fields

| Field            | Type              | Description                                           |
|------------------|-------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|
| `app_version`    | string (semver)   | Protocol version, e.g. "1.0.0"                       |
| `ai_generated`   | boolean           | true if content was generated/modified by AI          |
| `generator`      | object            | { platform: string, model: string }                   |
| `generated_at`   | string (ISO 8601) | Timestamp of generation completion                    |
| `generation_id`  | string (UUID v4)  | Globally unique identifier for this generation event  |

## Core Schema — Optional Fields

| Field                  | Type        | Description                                       |
|------------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------------|
| `inputs`               | object      | Description of inputs used for generation         |
| `review`               | object      | Human review status                               |
| `usage`                | object      | Token/resource usage                              |
| `verification_uri`     | string (URI)| Endpoint for third-party verification             |
| `parent_generation_id` | string (UUID)| Links to previous generation in a chain          |
| `content_hash`         | string      | SHA-256 hash for integrity verification           |
| `extensions`           | object      | Namespaced domain-specific metadata               |

## Minimal Example

```json
{
  "app_version": "1.0.0",
  "ai_generated": true,
  "generator": {
    "platform": "example-platform",
    "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4"
  },
  "generated_at": "2026-03-06T14:22:00Z",
  "generation_id": "a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890"
}
```

## Inline Embedding Example

```json
{
  "title": "Premium Leather Wallet",
  "description": "Handcrafted from full-grain leather...",
  "_ai_provenance": {
    "app_version": "1.0.0",
    "ai_generated": true,
    "generator": {
      "platform": "my-platform",
      "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4"
    },
    "generated_at": "2026-03-06T14:22:00Z",
    "generation_id": "a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890",
    "verification_uri": "https://verify.example.com/v1/verify",
    "review": { "human_reviewed": false }
  }
}
```

## HTTP Header Embedding

```
X-APP-Version: 1.0.0
X-APP-AI-Generated: true
X-APP-Generation-Id: a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890
X-APP-Generator-Platform: my-platform
X-APP-Generator-Model: anthropic/claude-sonnet-4
X-APP-Generated-At: 2026-03-06T14:22:00Z
X-APP-Verification-URI: https://verify.example.com/v1/verify
X-APP-Human-Reviewed: false
```

## Linked Metadata Embedding

```
Link: <https://verify.example.com/v1/metadata/a1b2c3d4-...>; rel="ai-provenance"; type="application/app+json"
```

## Verification Protocol

Level 1 — Generation ID Lookup:
```
GET {verification_uri}/{generation_id}
→ { "found": true, "ai_generated": true, "generator": {...}, "generated_at": "..." }
→ { "found": false }
```

Level 2 — Content Hash Matching:
```
POST {verification_uri}/match
Body: { "content_hash": "sha256:...", "content_type": "application/json" }
→ { "matches": [{...}] }
→ { "matches": [] }
```

Rules: No authentication required. Never returns content. Rate limiting recommended.

## Review Object (Human Oversight)

```json
{
  "review": {
    "human_reviewed": true,
    "reviewer_role": "editor",
    "reviewed_at": "2026-03-06T15:30:00Z",
    "review_type": "approved_without_changes"
  }
}
```

Review types: "approved_without_changes", "edited", "rejected", "pending".
Reviewer role records a functional role, not PII.

## Extensions

Namespaced domain-specific metadata using reverse-domain notation:

```json
{
  "extensions": {
    "org.c2pa": { "manifest_uri": "https://example.com/c2pa/manifest.cbor" },
    "io.modelcontextprotocol": { "server_name": "my-mcp-server", "tool_name": "generate" },
    "com.acme": { "tenant_id": "t-123" }
  }
}
```

## Content Hashing

Algorithm: SHA-256 (required), SHA-384 and SHA-512 (optional).
Format: "{algorithm}:{hex-hash}", e.g. "sha256:e3b0c44298fc1c14..."

For JSON: hash the canonical form (keys sorted, no whitespace, UTF-8, `_ai_provenance` key excluded).
For non-JSON: hash the raw bytes.

## Relationship to Other Standards

- C2PA: Complementary — C2PA covers images/video/audio, APP covers text/structured data
- MCP: Complementary — MCP connects AI to tools, APP records provenance of AI output
- Schema.org: Interoperable — APP coexists with CreativeWork metadata
- W3C PROV: Compatible — APP maps to the PROV data model
- IPTC: Complementary — APP extends provenance beyond photojournalism

## EU AI Act Article 50 Compliance

| Obligation                        | APP Feature                                    |
|-----------------------------------|------------------------------------------------|
| Machine-readable AI marking       | `ai_generated: true` in `_ai_provenance` metadata        |
| Detectable as AI-generated        | Three embedding modes                          |
| Free detection mechanism          | Public verification endpoints, no auth         |
| Deployer disclosure               | `ai_generated` field for downstream systems    |
| Human review exemption            | `review.human_reviewed` with timestamp         |
| Non-breaking marking              | `_ai_provenance` key ignorable; headers non-breaking     |

## JSON Schema

Published at: https://aiprovenanceprotocol.io/schema/v1.0/app-metadata.schema.json
Repository: spec/v1.0/schema/app-metadata.schema.json

## MCP Integration

MCP servers generating AI content should include APP metadata in tool responses.
Use the `io.modelcontextprotocol` extension namespace for MCP-specific context.
Use `parent_generation_id` to create provenance chains across multi-step MCP workflows.
